role_title: Senior Manager, Consumer Discovery - Strategy & Operations

seniority.level: Senior Manager (IC or people-manager at director-minus-one level)
level_signals: 8+ years of experience in high-performance cultures
Reports into a Director on Marketplace Strategy & Operations team
Comp range $170K–$250K base consistent with Senior Manager at DoorDash

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of experience. You've succeeded in high-performance cultures (whether in tech, operations, strategy, consulting, banking, startups or a related experience).
You have 2+ years of experience managing teams.
You've built, launched, or scaled AI-powered consumer products, and you can help raise the bar for how our team builds, makes decisions and creates more magical customer experiences.
You solve problems from first principles. You're excited to solve problems in innovative ways, and you resist "this is how it's always been done" thinking.
You are able to identify consumer pain points, lead with the consumer front of mind and build go to market strategy to enhance our consumer experience.
You let data win arguments, and you're comfortable pulling your own data in SQL or modeling in Excel.
You're unafraid of ambiguity. Rapid change and learning is exciting to you, and you thrive in ambiguous, startup environments

You're equally comfortable operating at 10,000 feet and 1 foot. You don't hesitate to get in the weeds and operate at the lowest level of detail, but you're just as comfortable thinking long-term and inspiring a team.
You'll work with a cross-functional team, including marketing, design, product, and operations.
You'll transform strategies into operational experiments that create measurable and actionable outcomes, informing your larger initiative implementation plans.

scope.surface: Consumer Discovery — in-app experiences across DoorDash marketplace (Restaurants, New Verticals, Emerging Bets)
primary_metrics_inferred: Consumer engagement with in-app discovery features
Operational and financial impact of experiments
core_responsibilities: Define, execute, and build in-app experiences for the entire marketplace
Bring AI-forward solutions into the DoorDash consumer app
Refine go-to-market playbook for consumer discovery
Develop strategy from first principles for best serving customers
Transform strategies into operational experiments with measurable outcomes
Assess operational and financial impacts of decisions and iterate based on data
Manage a team of direct and indirect reports

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($170K–$250K base)
Clear product surface and ownership (consumer app, full marketplace)
Role has genuine AI mandate ('AI leader' framing is substantive, not decorative)
Comprehensive benefits package described in detail
Inclusive hiring statement and accommodation process mentioned
red_flags: Work location type not stated — unclear whether remote, hybrid, or onsite; no cities listed for the role itself
Visa sponsorship policy not addressed in JD
Salary range is very wide ($80K spread), making it hard to gauge level targeting without recruiter conversation
No explicit product metrics or OKRs described, making scope and success criteria ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirements, management scope, comp range, and reporting structure)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'base salary is localized according to an employee's work location')
ambiguities: Work location is not specified in the JD body or metadata — the role could be remote, hybrid, or onsite. No cities are named for this specific role (San Francisco Fair Chance and LA ordinance references are legal boilerplate, not location indicators).
The NYC Covey AEDT notice implies the role may be associated with a NYC office, but this is not confirmed as the primary or required location.
Nice-to-haves section is empty because all 'We're excited about you because…' bullets use unconditional language and have been classified as hard requirements.
